$description: DRAFT — captured from components/ui/table.tsx. Not consumed at runtime yet (Phase 4). rowHeight is the density-mode hook (compact/comfortable).

headerHeight.$description: Table header is a row, not a touch target — pinned to 40px when size.control.xl moved to 44.

headerFontWeight.$description: The header is the only row in the table that is not data — weight is what says so.

row-selected-background.$description: A selected row is a 10% BRAND wash — not muted grey. Selection is a decision the user made; hover is just where the mouse happens to be, so the two must not share a colour. 10% is enough to see across a long table and light enough to keep the text at full contrast.

row-hover-surface.$description: Hover — half-strength muted. Alpha-baked in Figma; recompute if --muted moves.

footer-surface.$description: The totals row. Same 50% wash as hover so it reads as part of the table, not as a second header.

cell-control-background.$description: Inputs and selects are bg-transparent by default, which is right on a page but WRONG in a table: the moment a row is hovered or selected, the field takes on the row wash and stops reading as a field — the border is left floating on a tinted block. Inside a cell they get an opaque background so the control stays a control no matter what the row is doing.

rowBorderWidth.$description: Every row carries its own bottom border — the LAST one drops it, so the table does not end in a line that looks like a broken row.

sortIconSize.$description: The sort affordance. Present only on columns that CAN be sorted — a sort icon on every column teaches people it means nothing.

cellMinHeight.$description: Every cell is at least 48px tall, whatever it holds. Without this the row height follows its tallest cell — a text cell is 32, a cell with an input is 44, one with a two-line value is 52 — and the rows come out ragged, so the eye has to re-find every column on the way down. 48 also clears the touch floor for the controls that live in cells.
